-- 6 of 66 --

Formatting As You Go?
You Have 2 Op%ons
(1) You can build your enTre 3XVSL Script in Word and
then transfer it over to PowerPoint, Keynote, or a
Google PresentaTon when you’re finished
PROS:
• 	You can see then enTre script all at once and make sure it flows
• 	You can easily move elements around
• 	You are forced to focus more on the content and less on the
layout, which ensures that your copy is powerful and persuasive
CONS:
• 	You have to transfer it all to a Powerpoint, Keynote, or Google
PresentaTon File when you’re finished
• 	Which may mean that you have to copy it all one line at a Tme

-- 7 of 66 --

Formatting As You Go?
You Have 2 Op%ons
(2) You can write the enTre VSL right within your
PowerPoint, Keynote, or Google PresentaTon
Document 	as you go 	(Least Preferred OpTon)
PROS:
• 	All of the script is in your PowerPoint File when you’re
finished so there is minimal copy/paste
CONS:
• 	You will focus less on how to build the content and more
on typing, cut/paste from your previous homework, etc.

-- 12 of 66 --

Let’s Start With Some Tips
When creaTng a winning VSL, it pays to format
your text using the Title Case Technique. This creates the
appearance of headlines
Remember: You should test this! So be sure you make
two copies of your VSL if you want to test it; one with
Title Case, one with normal case
A website that will help you automaTcally format blocks
of text into Title Case:
h.p://Tnyurl.com/MakeTitleCase

-- 13 of 66 --

Let’s Start With Some Tips
The Ac%on Drop
This is a technique I created to push the reader forward
visually and from an NLP perspecTve
You “drop” a hint to bring the reader’s focus back into the
present moment by using words to prompt the user to take
ac%on within the moment
Today
Now
You See
Listen
Actually

-- 14 of 66 --

Let’s Start With Some Tips
The Ac%on Drop: Examples
“I Have To Warn You First:”
versus
“Now, I Have To Warn You First:”
“You Know You Need A Plan If You Really Want To
Make More Money Online…”
versus
“Listen: You Know You Need A Plan If You Really
Want To Make More Money Online…”

-- 15 of 66 --

Let’s Start With Some Tips
Avoid The Word “But”
“But” is a NLP stop term. It halts the reader
in the moment. This can be a good thing, but
(ha) you need to know how and where to use it
Instead, use these words:
Yet
However
And

-- 16 of 66 --

Let’s Start With Some Tips
Example: Avoiding The Word “But”
“But Instead, I Want You To Imagine…”
versus
“However, Instead I Want You To Imagine…”
“This Has All The Advantages, But Without…”
versus
“This Has All The Advantages, And Without…”

-- 17 of 66 --

Let’s Start With Some Tips
Avoid “The Assump%on”
You do not know what your reader is thinking, and suggesTng
that you do can come off as insulTng on a psychological level
When you say, “I Know What You Are Thinking”
you risk two things:
(1) The viewer wasn’t thinking anything related, but now he/
she is thinking about it. If what you’re menToning is negaTve,
this can put them out of state
(2) You risk offense by suggesTng even slightly that you can
read their minds, or know them be.er than they know
themselves

-- 18 of 66 --

Let’s Start With Some Tips
Alterna%ve To “The Assump%on”
Use The “May/OR” Technique, and feel free to
combine it with an AcTon Drop
Example:
“I Know You May Be Thinking, “But I’ve Already
Seen This Before…”
versus
“Now, You May Be Thinking That You’ve Heard This
Before… OR Perhaps You Already Know This Is
Totally New And Different…”

-- 22 of 66 --

Step 1: The Snap Suggestion
Op%onal Introduc%on
Use if you are 	the person behind the
product or service
Do not use if you are selling something where
you are not the “face” of the
product or service
Example:
“Hi, I’m Jon…”

-- 23 of 66 --

Step 1: The Snap Suggestion
PaLern Interrupt
A Pa.ern Interrupt disengages your
viewer’s mind momentarily and grabs their
a.enTon via curiosity (think of snapping
your fingers during a conversaTon)
I’ll cover some examples, and then we’ll
cover the anatomy of creaTng a Pa.ern
Interrupt

Step 1: The Snap Suggestion
The Big Promise
The “Sugges9on” Part Of The Equa9on
Your only goal is to encourage the viewer
to watch the video unTl the end. You
achieve this by (1) giving the viewer a
suggesTon while in the state of pa.ern
interrupt, and (2) ethically bribing them to
watch the video unTl the end.

Step 1: The Snap Suggestion
Video Scarcity
Get Your Viewer To Watch 	Now
Use actual (real) scarcity — Let the viewer
know your video will not be online “forever”
Other examples of wording:
“I don’t know how long I’ll have this online…”
“I don’t know how long this will be 	free…”

-- 49 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
Begin Building Deep Rapport With Your Viewer
Good News: You’ve already done 90% of this!
You will be using your Nightmare Story for
most of this secTon
Vital Connec%on Formula
Modest IntroducTon
Nightmare Story
TransiTon Into The Big Problem (Step 3)
About “The System”
Results From The System
Final TransiTon Phrase

-- 50 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
The Modest Introduc%on
List your credenTals and experTse in a 	modest
way 	by using the “I wasn’t always…” technique:
Example
“I wasn’t always a bestselling fitness author…
and I certainly wasn’t always a guy you’d see on
the cover of a fitness magazine!...”

-- 51 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
The Modest Introduc%on
Tips
Do not use pictures of you… yet.
DO use social proof pictures. For instance, in my example
below, I may include a magazine featuring me on the
inside (not on the cover), or the logo of magazines that
people might know. This is social proof.
Example
“I wasn’t always a bestselling fitness author… and I
certainly wasn’t always a guy you’d see on the cover of a
fitness magazine!...”

-- 52 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
Your Nightmare Story
Simply insert your enTre Nightmare Story into the VSL at
this point, using the 1 Sentence Technique— one
sentence per slide, followed by and ellipse (…)
Every 5-­‐7 slides, use the Paraphrase Technique and sum
up your sentence using 3-­‐5 words to break the pa.ern of
every word appearing on the slide. This will hold the
viewer’s interest.
Examples of these techniques are found within your
PowerPoint Template in the members area

-- 53 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
Transi%on To The Big Problem
Begin your transiTon into Step 3 (The Big Problem)
simply by reminding your viewer that you are like
them, only you have a “system”
(or formula, plan, strategy, blueprint, etc.)
Example
“Remember, despite my success today, I’m no
different than you… I’m just a guy who happened to
stumble on a system…”

-- 54 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
About The System
Here is where you want to insert an image, but
not an image of your product, but rather an
image that represents the success or benefits
your product delivers.
On this slide you will want to use an inclusion
statement to further connect you to your
viewer (deepen the rapport even further)

-- 55 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
About The System
Example
“This is a System (or alternate term) that allows
people like us 	to experience…”

-- 56 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
About The System
This is followed by 3 results of your product, each of
which are posiTve in nature
Example
“Live a life free from the burdens of endless weight
gain…” “Enjoy the freedom of eaTng foods we enjoy and
sTll losing weight…” “And experiencing the excitement of
seeing the body of our dreams in the bathroom mirror!”
No%ce the Wordsmithing!
Freedom; experiencing; excitement; using “bathroom
mirror” as an anchor (everyone has one)

-- 57 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
Results Of The System
Personalize, then share the experience of the
results you’ve experienced using this System
Example
“This is the same system that’s allowed me to
effortlessly drop 78 pounds of blubber and keep it
off for more than a decade…”
“And it’s the System I’ll be sharing with you today
so you can finally see the results you desire, too!”

-- 58 of 66 --

Step 2: The Vital Connection
Final Transi%on Phrase
A direct lead-­‐in to The Big Problem that also
opens another loop
Example
“And, I’ll share all the specifics of that System with
you in just a moment… First, I must tell you about
the real problem we face…”
“This is the hidden foe behind why you are gaining
weight in the first place… and most people have no
idea that it’s not their fault!...”
